query: I'm running k-means clustering on customer segments. Audit my train/test split.
expected_behavior: Does NOT criticize the user for not having a train/test split (does NOT claim it is a leakage or methodological failure)
Names cluster-stability / bootstrap-silhouette / gap-statistic / ARI-under-perturbation as an appropriate validation approach for unsupervised clustering
Clarifies that the train/test split paradigm is a supervised-learning concept and does not transfer directly to k-means
